parts.title: School Librarians
prompts: Give me suggestions on how I can support students in [grades 1–3] with early literacy development through interactive read-aloud sessions and book selection.
Create a plan for a writing project for [upper primary students] where they write short stories inspired by books in the library's collection.
How can I help Upper Secondary students improve their academic writing using library resources, such as guides on argumentation and structure?
Suggest 5 easy-to-read books in Swedish for [lower primary students] with reading difficulties, including activities to strengthen their reading comprehension.
Give me a lesson plan for supporting newly arrived students in [grades 7–9] in developing their Swedish language skills through reading and writing.
Create a list of 8 books that can help students in [grades 4–6] develop their reading comprehension by analysing characters and themes.
How can I use library resources to support students with Swedish as a second language in their writing process?
Suggest a project where [upper primary students] write book reviews to be published on the school website or in a library newsletter.
Give me ideas for how I can create a writing workshop for [lower primary students] where they create their own stories based on picture books.
Create a strategy to help students in [grades 4–6] improve their reading engagement by connecting reading to their interests. Information Literacy and Source Criticism
How can I teach students in grade [5] about source criticism in an engaging way? Give me a lesson plan with practical exercises.
Create a checklist for [upper primary students] on how to evaluate an internet source using source-critical principles, adapted to [reference to current curriculum].
Give me a lesson plan for [grades 7–9] on how to search for information effectively in library databases and on the internet.
Suggest 5 interactive activities for teaching [lower primary students] the basics of source criticism, such as distinguishing between facts and opinions.
Create a simple source criticism checklist suitable for [grades 4–6].
Create a guide for students in grade [8] on how to cite sources correctly in APA style, with examples.
Give me suggestions on how I can use news articles from Swedish newspapers to teach source criticism to [Upper Secondary students].
How can I introduce [upper primary students] to academic articles and databases such as NE or Libris?
Create a workshop for teachers on how they can use library resources to strengthen students' information literacy.
Give me a scenario where I can teach students in grade [4] to distinguish between advertising and information on the internet.
Give me a lesson plan for grade [6] on how to identify credible sources on the internet, with a focus on distinguishing between news and disinformation.
Give me a lesson plan for grades [7–9] on how to critically evaluate responses from chatbots using source-critical methods.
How can I teach [lower primary students] the basics of information searching in a playful way, for example by using picture books?
Suggest a scenario where I can teach [Upper Secondary students] to analyse the credibility of social media posts, with practical exercises.
Create a guide for grade [7] on how to search for information in both print and digital sources, with a focus on effective search techniques.
Give me a lesson plan for teaching students in grade [5] about copyright and plagiarism when using information from the internet.
How can I collaborate with history teachers to teach source criticism in connection with a project on World War II for grade [9]?
Suggest 5 practical exercises for teaching [grades 4–6 students] to distinguish between primary and secondary sources.
Create a checklist for [Upper Secondary students] on how to assess a source based on currency, relevance, and authorship.
Give me ideas for how I can use podcasts and video clips to teach source criticism to [upper primary students]. Reading Promotion and Literature Outreach

Give me suggestions for 10 new Swedish books for young adults from 2024 and 2025 that are suitable for [upper primary students], including a brief description of each book and how I can use them in a reading promotion project.
Suggest 5 ways to attract reluctant readers in [grades 7–9] to fiction, including book recommendations and activities.
Create a presentation on the importance of reading for students in [grades 4–6], with statistics and concrete examples of how reading benefits their development.
Suggest a list of 8 easy-to-read books in Swedish for newly arrived students in [grades 6–9], focusing on stories that reflect their experiences.

Suggest 10 Swedish children's and young adult books from the past three years that address themes of friendship, including a brief summary and suggestions for how they can be used in the classroom for [grades 4–6].
Create a plan for a reading promotion campaign for [lower primary] using fairy tales and folk tales to spark an interest in reading.
How can I create a reading project for [Upper Secondary students] that connects classic Swedish literature to contemporary themes such as identity and social change?
Create a concept for a school-wide 'reading challenge' where students across different grades read books in various genres over one term.
How can I create a read-aloud session for [grades 2–3] that combines a picture book with a movement activity to keep students engaged?

Create a plan for a themed week on science fiction literature for [upper primary], with book recommendations and connections to science subjects. Collaboration with Teachers and Curriculum Integration
How can I collaborate with Swedish language teachers to integrate fiction into teaching about the history of the language for grade [8]?
Give me a suggestion for how I can support science teachers with library resources for a project on climate change in grade [7].
Create a lesson plan where the library is used to support a cross-curricular project on human rights for [Upper Secondary].
Suggest how I can collaborate with art teachers to create a project where students illustrate scenes from a book they have read.
Create a suggestion for how I can support mother-tongue teachers with literature and resources for multilingual students.
Give me a lesson plan where the library is used to support a project on Nordic mythology for grade [6], in collaboration with Swedish language teachers.
Suggest how I can help technology teachers integrate library resources into a project on artificial intelligence for [Upper Secondary].
Create a plan for how I can collaborate with music teachers to create a project where students connect song lyrics to fiction.
Give me ideas for how I can support teachers in using library resources to strengthen students' writing skills in Swedish.

Give me suggestions for how I can collaborate with Social Studies teachers to create a project on migration and integration, with a focus on literature and source criticism. Inclusion and Accessibility
Give me suggestions for how I can adapt the library's collection for students with visual impairments, including tips on accessible formats such as audio books.
Create a plan for how I can make the library more welcoming for newly arrived students through multilingual literature and activities.
How can I collaborate with special education teachers to create reading promotion activities for students with neurodevelopmental conditions, such as autism or ADHD?
Suggest 5 books in Swedish suitable for students with dyslexia, along with strategies for making reading more accessible.
Give me ideas for how I can create an inclusive book club for students with different linguistic and cultural backgrounds in grades [7–9].
Create a checklist to ensure that the library environment is physically and cognitively accessible for all students.
How can I use signs as support (augmentative communication) in a read-aloud session for [lower primary students] with communication difficulties?
Suggest a project where the library is used to promote inclusion through literature about disability and neurodiversity for [grades 4–6].
Give me strategies for reaching students who avoid the library due to social or language barriers.
Create a plan for how I can collaborate with mother-tongue teachers to offer stories in students' home languages in the library. Digital Tools and Innovation
How can I use AI tools to create interactive reading promotion activities for [grades 4–6 students], for example by generating stories or quizzes?
Suggest 5 digital platforms or apps that are popular in Sweden and that I can use to create virtual book clubs for [upper primary students].
Create a plan for how I can introduce students in grades [4–6] to creating their own digital stories using library resources.
Give me ideas for how I can use QR codes in the library to guide students to book recommendations or digital resources.
How can I create a virtual tour of the school library to show new students how they can use our resources?
Suggest a project where [Upper Secondary students] use the library's digital tools to create podcasts based on books they have read.
Create a guide for how I can use social media such as Instagram or TikTok to reach [upper primary students] with book recommendations.
Give me a lesson plan for grade [7] on how to use the library's e-book platforms, such as Världens Bibliotek or Bibblix.
How can I integrate coding into a reading promotion project for [grades 4–6], for example by creating interactive stories?
Suggest how I can use augmented reality (AR) or virtual reality (VR) to make the library more engaging for [lower primary students]. Community Engagement and Current Awareness
Suggest how I can create a project where [upper primary students] use the library to explore local community issues through literature and sources.
Create a plan for a themed day on freedom of expression and censorship, connected to literature and current events, for [Upper Secondary].
Give me ideas for how I can connect the library's activities to the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, such as gender equality or peaceful societies.
How can I collaborate with local organisations, such as sports clubs, to promote reading among students outside of school?
Suggest a project where the library is used to teach students in grade [6] about the role of the media in society, with a focus on source criticism.
Create a plan for how I can involve parents in the library's reading promotion activities, for example through workshops or book recommendations.
Give me suggestions for how I can use library resources to raise awareness of national minority languages and cultures.
How can I create a collaboration with local museums or cultural institutions to connect literature to local history for [grades 4–6]?
Suggest how I can organise an event where students discuss current social issues based on books they have read.
Create a strategy for how I can use library resources to promote global awareness, for example through literature on climate change. Library Environment and Events
Give me ideas for creating a welcoming and inspiring library environment for [lower and middle primary students].
How can I organise a 'book swap day' for students in grades [7–9] to promote reading and sustainability?
Create a proposal for an author visit for [upper primary], including how I prepare students and follow up after the visit.
Give me a plan for a 'read all day' activity for the whole school, with suggested activities for different grade levels.
Suggest 5 ways to use social media to promote the school library's activities to students and parents.
Suggest how I can create a quiet reading space in the library for students with special needs, such as those with neurodevelopmental conditions.
Suggest how I can create a 'maker space' corner in the school library for creative activities connected to literature for [grades 4–6].
How can I create an exhibition in the library about Swedish Nobel Prize laureates in literature, with interactive elements for students?
Suggest 5 ways to make the school library more accessible for students with reading and writing difficulties, such as dyslexia.
Create a concept for a 'library detective' activity where [lower primary students] solve mysteries connected to books and the library. Professional Development
Give me a suggestion for how I can organise a professional development day for school librarians in my municipality on reading promotion strategies.
How can I create a network for school librarians in Sweden to share resources and ideas, for example via digital platforms?
Suggest 5 ways I can stay up to date on new children's and young adult books published in Sweden.
Create a plan for how I can develop my own skills in using new digital tools for library work, such as databases or AI.
Give me a lesson plan for training teachers on how they can integrate library resources into their teaching in accordance with [reference to current curriculum].
How can I participate in national or international library conferences to develop my skills as a school librarian?
Suggest how I can create a presentation on the school library's role for school leadership in order to secure more resources.
Give me ideas for how I can collaborate with public libraries to strengthen my professional development as a school librarian.
Create a guide for how I can document and evaluate the library's reading promotion efforts to demonstrate results to school leadership.
How can I use research on reading and information literacy to improve my work as a school librarian? Library Administration and Resource Management
Give me a strategy for inventorying and updating the library's book collection to ensure it is relevant and diverse.
How can I create an efficient routine for managing loans and returns in a busy school library?
Suggest how I can use the library's budget to prioritise the purchase of new books and digital resources.
Create a guide for how I can organise the library's shelves according to the Dewey or SAB classification system to make it easier for students to navigate.
Give me ideas for how I can digitalise parts of the library's administration, such as book orders or loan statistics.
How can I create a system for collecting feedback from students and teachers about the library's collection and services?
Suggest a plan for how I can collaborate with other school libraries in the municipality to share resources and reduce costs.
Create a checklist to ensure that the library's collection meets the requirements for diversity and inclusion in accordance with [reference to current curriculum].
Give me strategies for handling damaged or lost books in the library's collection in an efficient way.
How can I create an annual report on the library's activities to demonstrate its value to school leadership?
